swc.Name: SWC_ElectricPowerSteering
swc.Description_PseudoCode: Application SWC providing assist torque generation, safety supervision, initialization, and mode management for EPS. Implements AUTOSAR SWC with ASIL-D decomposition. Contains 4 runnables mapped to 2 OsTask periods.
swc.Error_Handling: DEM integration for all faults; FiM permission gating

input_ports.Name: VehicleSpeed
input_ports.Description_PseudoCode: Vehicle speed received from CAN network via COM stack. Signal name: Veh_Speed_CAN. Received via SenderReceiver interface from BswM/ComStack.
input_ports.Error_Handling: Range check in RE_MonitorSafety; NaN detection

input_ports.Name: SteeringAngle
input_ports.Description_PseudoCode: Steering wheel angle from angle sensor SWC via SenderReceiver. Signal: SteeringAngle_deg. Used for rate-of-change plausibility.
input_ports.Error_Handling: Out-of-range -> FaultFlag; rate check: d(angle)/dt < 1000 deg/s
input_ports.Notes: Cross-check with motor position sensor if available

input_ports.Name: DriverTorque
input_ports.Description_PseudoCode: Torque sensor reading applied by driver. Dual-channel sensor reading recommended; plausibility between channel A and B shall be < 0.2 Nm.
input_ports.Error_Handling: Dual-channel plausibility; NaN check; gradient limit: 50 Nm/s max
input_ports.Notes: Safety-critical; ASIL-D decomposition if dual channel

input_ports.Name: EPS_Mode
input_ports.Description_PseudoCode: Operating mode command from Vehicle Mode Manager (VMM) via SenderReceiver. Values: 0=Off, 1=Init, 2=Assist, 3=FailSafe.
input_ports.Error_Handling: Undefined value (>3) -> default to FailSafe; log DEM event
input_ports.Notes: Mode change debounce: 2 consecutive identical readings

output_ports.Name: AssistTorque
output_ports.Description_PseudoCode: Computed assist torque request sent to motor control SWC. Written by RE_ControlTorque; overridden to 0.0 by safety runnable on fault.
output_ports.Error_Handling: Force to 0.0 if FaultFlag=1; ramp-down within 200ms
output_ports.Notes: Rate-limit on AssistTorque change: max 20 Nm/s gradient

output_ports.Name: EPS_Status
output_ports.Description_PseudoCode: System health status broadcast to gateway and instrument cluster. 0=OK, 1=Degraded (reduced assist), 2=Fault (no assist).
output_ports.Error_Handling: Status sticky until cleared by Init sequence; logged via DEM
output_ports.Notes: CAN transmission period: 10ms; signal timeout monitored by gateway

output_ports.Name: FaultFlag
output_ports.Description_PseudoCode: Boolean safety flag indicating an active fault. Set by RE_MonitorSafety, read by RE_ControlTorque to suppress assist output.
output_ports.Error_Handling: Once set, cleared only by validated re-init sequence
output_ports.Notes: Latched fault: cannot self-clear without ignition cycle

internal_vars.Name: assist_factor
internal_vars.Description_PseudoCode: Interpolated assist gain from speed-assist lookup table. Range: 0.0 (min assist at high speed) to 3.0 (max assist at low speed).

internal_vars.Name: fault_counter
internal_vars.Description_PseudoCode: Counts consecutive cycles where a fault condition is detected. Fault is confirmed after 2 cycles; escalated to EPS_Fault after 100ms.
internal_vars.Error_Handling: Overflow protection: clamp at 255; reset on valid state

internal_vars.Name: eps_state
internal_vars.Description_PseudoCode: Internal state machine state. States: INIT(0), ASSIST_READY(1), ASSIST_ACTIVE(2), FAILSAFE(3), OFF(4).
internal_vars.AUTOSAR_Concept: Static local variable / shared via IRV

internal_vars.Name: raw_torque
internal_vars.Description_PseudoCode: Intermediate computed torque before clamping. raw_torque = DriverTorque * assist_factor.
internal_vars.Error_Handling: Clamped to MAX_TORQUE before writing to AssistTorque

calib_params.Name: MAX_TORQUE
calib_params.Description_PseudoCode: Maximum allowable assist torque magnitude. Symmetric limit applied to both positive and negative AssistTorque.
calib_params.Error_Handling: Violation -> DEM fault; value range checked at startup
calib_params.Notes: Stored in NVRAM; loaded during RE_Initialize

calib_params.Name: SpeedAssistCurve
calib_params.Description_PseudoCode: Lookup table: VehicleSpeed [km/h] -> assist_factor [dimensionless]. N_POINTS=10. Linearly interpolated between breakpoints.
calib_params.Error_Handling: Values validated at startup: monotonically decreasing factor

calib_params.Name: TORQUE_LIMIT_MAX
calib_params.Description_PseudoCode: Safety monitor torque threshold. If abs(AssistTorque) exceeds this, FaultFlag is set. Shall be >= MAX_TORQUE + 0.5 Nm margin.

calib_params.Name: FAULT_ESCALATE_MS
calib_params.Description_PseudoCode: Time in milliseconds before a confirmed fault escalates to full fault state and AssistTorque is forced to 0.

runnables.Name: RE_ControlTorque
runnables.Description_PseudoCode:
Pseudo-code:
// [1] Guard: mode check
IF eps_state != ASSIST_ACTIVE:
    AssistTorque = 0.0
    RETURN

// [2] Read inputs via RTE
speed   = Rte_IRead_PpVehicleSpeed_DeVehicleSpeed()
torque  = Rte_IRead_PpDriverTorque_DeDriverTorque()
fault   = Rte_IrvRead_FaultFlag()

// [3] Fault gate
IF fault == TRUE:
    AssistTorque = 0.0
    RETURN

// [4] Speed-based assist lookup (interpolate calibration curve)
assist_factor = Interp1D(SpeedAssistCurve, speed)
assist_factor = CLAMP(assist_factor, 0.0, 3.0)

// [5] Raw torque computation
raw_torque = torque * assist_factor

// [6] Symmetric torque clamping
AssistTorque = CLAMP(raw_torque, -MAX_TORQUE, MAX_TORQUE)

// [7] Rate limiter (anti-jerk)
delta = AssistTorque - prev_AssistTorque
IF ABS(delta) > MAX_TORQUE_RATE * TASK_PERIOD_S:
    AssistTorque = prev_AssistTorque + SIGN(delta)*MAX_TORQUE_RATE*TASK_PERIOD_S

// [8] Write output
Rte_IWrite_PpAssistTorque_DeAssistTorque(AssistTorque)
prev_AssistTorque = AssistTorque
runnables.Port_Interface: R: PpVehicleSpeed, PpDriverTorque; P: PpAssistTorque; IRV: FaultFlag
runnables.Error_Handling: On fault: set AssistTorque=0; return without further computation

runnables.Name: RE_MonitorSafety
runnables.Description_PseudoCode:
Pseudo-code:
// [1] Read all signals
speed  = Rte_IRead_PpVehicleSpeed()
torque_in = Rte_IRead_PpDriverTorque()
angle  = Rte_IRead_PpSteeringAngle()
assist = Rte_IrvRead_AssistTorque()

local_fault = FALSE

// [2] Input range plausibility
IF IS_NAN(torque_in) OR torque_in < -10.0 OR torque_in > 10.0:
    local_fault = TRUE
    Dem_ReportErrorStatus(DEM_EVENT_DRIVER_TORQUE_SENSOR_FAULT, PREFAILED)

IF speed < 0.0 OR speed > 250.0:
    local_fault = TRUE
    Dem_ReportErrorStatus(DEM_EVENT_VEH_SPEED_SIGNAL_LOSS, PREFAILED)

IF angle < -720.0 OR angle > 720.0:
    local_fault = TRUE
    Dem_ReportErrorStatus(DEM_EVENT_STEERING_ANGLE_PLAUSIBILITY, PREFAILED)

// [3] Output torque limit check
IF ABS(assist) > TORQUE_LIMIT_MAX:
    local_fault = TRUE
    Dem_ReportErrorStatus(DEM_EVENT_ASSIST_TORQUE_SATURATION, PREFAILED)

// [4] Redundancy / debounce logic
IF local_fault == TRUE:
    fault_counter = MIN(fault_counter + 1, 255)
ELSE:
    fault_counter = MAX(fault_counter - 1, 0)

// [5] Fault confirmation (2 consecutive cycles)
IF fault_counter >= FAULT_CONFIRM_CYCLES:
    FaultFlag = TRUE
    EPS_Status = DEGRADED
ELSE:
    FaultFlag = FALSE

// [6] Fault escalation (>100ms = 10 cycles at 10ms)
IF fault_counter >= (FAULT_ESCALATE_MS / 10):
    EPS_Status = FAULT
    Rte_IWrite_PpAssistTorque(0.0)   // safety override

// [7] Write outputs
Rte_IWrite_PpFaultFlag(FaultFlag)
Rte_IWrite_PpEPS_Status(EPS_Status)
runnables.Port_Interface: R: PpVehicleSpeed, PpDriverTorque, PpSteeringAngle; P: PpFaultFlag, PpEPS_Status; IRV: AssistTorque
runnables.Error_Handling: DEM reporting; force AssistTorque=0 on escalation; FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque
runnables.Trace_Requirement: Independent of RE_ControlTorque; higher WCET budget 1ms

runnables.Name: RE_Initialize
runnables.Description_PseudoCode:
Pseudo-code:
// [1] Zero all outputs immediately
Rte_IWrite_PpAssistTorque(0.0)
Rte_IWrite_PpFaultFlag(FALSE)
Rte_IWrite_PpEPS_Status(OK)

// [2] Reset internal state variables
fault_counter      = 0
prev_AssistTorque  = 0.0
eps_state          = INIT

// [3] Initialize OS timers and watchdog
WdgM_SetMode(WDGM_MODE_SUPERVISED)
OsTimer_Start(TIMER_SAFETY_MONITOR)

// [4] Load calibration tables from NVRAM
NvM_ReadBlock(NVM_BLOCK_EPS_CALIB, &EPS_Calib)
IF NvM_GetErrorStatus() != NVM_REQ_OK:
    Use_Default_Calibration()
    Dem_ReportErrorStatus(DEM_EVENT_CALIB_LOAD_FAIL, FAILED)

// [5] Validate calibration
IF NOT Validate_SpeedAssistCurve(SpeedAssistCurve):
    Use_Default_Calibration()
    Dem_ReportErrorStatus(DEM_EVENT_CALIB_INVALID, FAILED)

// [6] Transition to ASSIST_READY
eps_state = ASSIST_READY
Rte_IWrite_PpEPS_Status(OK)

// [7] Signal init complete to BswM
BswM_RequestMode(EPS_INIT_DONE)
runnables.Port_Interface: P: PpAssistTorque, PpFaultFlag, PpEPS_Status; BswM port
runnables.Error_Handling: NvM read failure -> use ROM defaults; report DEM; continue
runnables.Trace_Requirement: Must complete within 200ms of OS start

runnables.Name: RE_HandleModeChange
runnables.Description_PseudoCode:
Pseudo-code:
// [1] Read new mode (event-triggered by data receive)
new_mode = Rte_IRead_PpEPS_Mode_DeEPS_Mode()

// [2] Debounce: require 2 consecutive same readings
IF new_mode != prev_mode_reading:
    debounce_counter = 0
    prev_mode_reading = new_mode
    RETURN
debounce_counter++
IF debounce_counter < MODE_DEBOUNCE_COUNT:
    RETURN
debounce_counter = 0

// [3] Validate mode value
IF new_mode > 3:
    new_mode = FAILSAFE   // default to safe state
    Dem_ReportErrorStatus(DEM_EVENT_EPS_MODE_INVALID, FAILED)

// [4] State machine transition
SWITCH (new_mode):
    CASE OFF:
        eps_state = OFF
        Rte_IWrite_PpAssistTorque(0.0)
        Rte_IWrite_PpEPS_Status(OK)

    CASE INIT:
        eps_state = INIT
        RE_Initialize()     // call init sequence
        eps_state = ASSIST_READY

    CASE ASSIST:
        IF eps_state == ASSIST_READY OR eps_state == ASSIST_ACTIVE:
            eps_state = ASSIST_ACTIVE
        ELSE:
            // Cannot enter ASSIST from FAILSAFE without re-init
            Dem_ReportErrorStatus(DEM_EVENT_EPS_MODE_INVALID, PREFAILED)

    CASE FAILSAFE:
        eps_state = FAILSAFE
        Rte_IWrite_PpAssistTorque(0.0)
        Rte_IWrite_PpEPS_Status(FAULT)
        FaultFlag = TRUE
runnables.Trigger_Timing: Event (data-receive callback, within 5ms of mode change)
runnables.Port_Interface: R: PpEPS_Mode; P: PpAssistTorque, PpEPS_Status, PpFaultFlag
runnables.Error_Handling: Invalid mode -> FAILSAFE; undefined transition -> log DEM
runnables.Trace_Requirement: Response latency < 5ms from mode change trigger

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_VEH_SPEED_SIGNAL_LOSS
dem_events.Description_PseudoCode: Vehicle speed signal out of range or lost
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 3 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_DRIVER_TORQUE_SENSOR_FAULT
dem_events.Description_PseudoCode: Driver torque sensor NaN, out-of-range, or gradient fault
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 1 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_STEERING_ANGLE_PLAUSIBILITY
dem_events.Description_PseudoCode: Steering angle sensor out of range
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 3 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_ASSIST_TORQUE_SATURATION
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 2 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_EPS_SYSTEM_FAULT
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 1 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_EPS_DEGRADED
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 2 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_EPS_MODE_INVALID
dem_events.Description_PseudoCode: Invalid or illegal mode transition detected
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 2 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_CALIB_LOAD_FAIL
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 3 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_CALIB_INVALID
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 2 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_SELFTEST_FAIL
dem_events.Description_PseudoCode: EPS self-test (RAM/ROM CRC) failed at init
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 1 events

dem_events.Name: DEM_EVENT_RUNNABLE_TIMING_VIOLATION
dem_events.Error_Handling: FiM blocks RE_ControlTorque on priority 1 events

fim.Name: FiM_ControlTorquePermission
fim.Description_PseudoCode: FiM permission controlling whether RE_ControlTorque is allowed to write AssistTorque. Inhibited when ASIL-D fault events DEM-002 or DEM-005 are active.
fim.Error_Handling: If permission = INHIBITED: skip assist computation; set AssistTorque=0
fim.Notes: FiM configuration in FiM.config AUTOSAR module

wdgm.Name: WdgM_EPS_Supervision
wdgm.Description_PseudoCode: Watchdog Manager supervised entities for all EPS runnables. Each runnable has start/stop checkpoint. Failure -> WdgM triggers global reset.
wdgm.SW_CalibrationParameter: Timeout thresholds per runnable (1.5x nominal period)
wdgm.Error_Handling: WdgM triggers ECU reset on SE_EXPIRED; DEM event logged before reset
wdgm.Notes: WdgM configured per AUTOSAR WdgM specification

few_shot_prompts.input_port_example:
Port: VehicleSpeed (required)
  Interface: R-Port / SenderReceiverInterface
  RTE call:  Rte_IRead_RE_ControlTorque_PpVehicleSpeed_DeVehicleSpeed
  Data Type: float32  Range: 0.0 .. 250.0 km/h
  Trigger:   5ms cyclic  ASIL: ASIL-D
  Error handling: Range check in RE_MonitorSafety; NaN detection
  DEM event: DEM_EVENT_VEH_SPEED_SIGNAL_LOSS
  Notes: Signal timeout = 15ms; default=0.0 on timeout
few_shot_prompts.output_port_example:
Port: AssistTorque (provided)
  Interface: P-Port / SenderReceiverInterface
  RTE call:  Rte_IWrite_RE_ControlTorque_PpAssistTorque_DeAssistTorque
  Data Type: float32  Range: -10.0 .. 10.0 Nm
  Trigger:   5ms cyclic  ASIL: ASIL-D
  Error handling: Force to 0.0 if FaultFlag=1; ramp-down within 200ms
  DEM event: DEM_EVENT_ASSIST_TORQUE_SATURATION
  Notes: Rate-limit on AssistTorque change: max 20 Nm/s gradient
few_shot_prompts.runnable_example:
Runnable: RE_ControlTorque
  Trigger: 5ms cyclic (OsTask_5ms)  ASIL: ASIL-D
  Port Interface: R: PpVehicleSpeed, PpDriverTorque; P: PpAssistTorque; IRV: FaultFlag
  CalPrm: MAX_TORQUE; SpeedAssistCurve; MAX_TORQUE_RATE
  Error handling: On fault: set AssistTorque=0; return without further computation
  DEM event: 
  PSEUDO-CODE:
    Pseudo-code:
    // [1] Guard: mode check
    IF eps_state != ASSIST_ACTIVE:
        AssistTorque = 0.0
        RETURN
    // [2] Read inputs via RTE
    speed   = Rte_IRead_PpVehicleSpeed_DeVehicleSpeed()
    torque  = Rte_IRead_PpDriverTorque_DeDriverTorque()
    fault   = Rte_IrvRead_FaultFlag()
    // [3] Fault gate
    IF fault == TRUE:
        AssistTorque = 0.0
        RETURN
    // [4] Speed-based assist lookup (interpolate calibration curve)
    assist_factor = Interp1D(SpeedAssistCurve, speed)
    assist_factor = CLAMP(assist_factor, 0.0, 3.0)
    // [5] Raw torque computation
    raw_torque = torque * assist_factor
    // [6] Symmetric torque clamping
    AssistTorque = CLAMP(raw_torque, -MAX_TORQUE, MAX_TORQUE)
    // [7] Rate limiter (anti-jerk)
    delta = AssistTorque - prev_AssistTorque
    IF ABS(delta) > MAX_TORQUE_RATE * TASK_PERIOD_S:
        AssistTorque = prev_AssistTorque + SIGN(delta)*MAX_TORQUE_RATE*TASK_PERIOD_S
    // [8] Write output
    Rte_IWrite_PpAssistTorque_DeAssistTorque(AssistTorque)
    prev_AssistTorque = AssistTorque
few_shot_prompts.calib_param_example:
CalPrm: MAX_TORQUE
  Port: Rte_Prm_RE_ControlTorque_MAX_TORQUE()
  Data Type: float32
  Range: 5.0 .. 10.0 Nm
  Default: Default = 10.0 Nm; OEM-configurable
  Memory: .EPS_CONST_CALIB
  Notes: Stored in NVRAM; loaded during RE_Initialize
